title: carex scopulus subsp. scopulus
intro: Carex scopulus subsp. scopulus, also known as the rock sedge, is a subspecies of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial herb native to mountainous regions, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The rock sedge has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, thanks to its high content of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Carex scopulus subsp. scopulus include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
